name: Jamaica Blue Mountains

staple_foods.name: Arabica coffee

key_flavor_bioactives.caffeine_concentration: 9.0-11.5 mg/g dry bean [Jamaica Blue Mountain Arabica — Mussatto et al. 2011 Trends Food Sci 22:404; comparable to Colombian at similar altitude]
key_flavor_bioactives.polyphenol_content: 4200-4800 mg/100g CGA equivalents [Jamaica Blue Mountain 1400-1600m — Campa et al. 2012 J Agric Food Chem 60:3252; altitude-appropriate CGA for 1500m]

terpenes.linalool: 0.6-1.2 mg/L in brewed coffee [Blue Mountain washed process — Buffo & Cardelli-Freire 2004]

organic_acids.malic_acid: 0.70-1.00 g/100g dry bean [Jamaica Blue Mountain 1500m — Lingle 2011 SCAA; consistent with altitude-malic correlation]

staple_foods.cultivar: Coffea arabica (Typica variety — Jamaica Blue Mountain is exclusively Typica)
staple_foods.notes: Jamaica Blue Mountain is one of the most geographically controlled coffee origins globally: the 'Jamaica Blue Mountain' designation applies only to beans grown 1000-1500m in the Blue Mountain ridge. The variety is exclusively Typica (C. arabica var. typica), the oldest and most genetically uniform Arabica cultivar. This genetic consistency makes it a strong natural experiment anchor: Typica at 1500m vs Ethiopian/Colombian/Kenyan varieties introduces genetic noise, but within-Arabica comparison is the target. Polyphenol CGA values calibrated to altitude-CGA regression: 1500m should fall between Colombian (1600m, 4600) and Toraja (1100m, 4000).
data_sources: Mussatto et al. (2011) — Chemistry, pharmacology, and commercial utilization of coffee, Trends Food Sci 22:404
Campa et al. (2012) — Chlorogenic acids in coffee, J Agric Food Chem 60:3252

staple_foods.name: yellow yam (Jamaican, Dioscorea cayenensis)
staple_foods.notes: Yellow yam (Dioscorea cayenensis) is the primary carbohydrate in Jamaican highland and lowland cuisine. Greater yam (D. alata) also grown. Yam ground provisions are the cultural staple carb.

staple_foods.name: ackee and saltfish

primary_metabolites.protein_content: ~25 g/100g [saltfish component — dried and desalted; ackee contributes ~2.9 g/100g protein]
primary_metabolites.lipid_content: ~15 g/100g [ackee fat content — Blighia sapida arils]

umami_compounds.glutamate: present — dried fish umami, codfish free glutamate ~0.3 g/100g

staple_foods.notes: Ackee and saltfish is the national dish of Jamaica. Ackee (Blighia sapida) is a West African fruit introduced to Jamaica — the cream-colored arils are eaten when fully ripe (unripe ackee contains hypoglycin A, toxic). The ackee fat fraction (oleic + linoleic-dominant) is nutritionally significant.

staple_foods.name: coconut oil

primary_metabolites.lauric_acid: 44-49% of total fatty acids [Caribbean coastal coconut — standard coconut oil composition]

staple_foods.notes: Coconut oil is a widely used cooking fat in Jamaican and Caribbean cuisine, though the highlands use it less than the coastal zones.

staple_foods.name: rum (Jamaican pot still, sugarcane fermented)

organic_acids.lactic_acid: present — dunder pit fermentation component in Jamaican rum
organic_acids.acetic_acid: high — Jamaican pot still rum uses long fermentation (5-14 days) with dunder and muck

staple_foods.notes: Jamaican pot still rum uses high-ester fermentation with dunder (spent wash) and muck (bacterial starter) for extended fermentation. Produces among the highest ester concentrations of any rum style (>300 g/hL AA isoamyl acetate, ethyl acetate). One of the most chemically complex fermented products in the dataset alongside soy sauce and aged cheese.

known_processing_methods: jerk (allspice + Scotch bonnet marinade, pit smoking)

notes: Jamaica Blue Mountains (1000-1700m). Metamorphic ridge soils, high rainfall and mist. Key natural experiment RSU: Arabica coffee at 1500m — second lowest in the 5-RSU altitude gradient (RSU-47 at 1100m, RSU-48 at 1500m, RSU-18 at 1600m, RSU-27 at 1800m, RSU-17 at 2200m). Expected polyphenol CGA ~4450 mg/100g — higher than Toraja (4000) and slightly below Colombian (4600). Yam variety and Jamaican rum fermentation are regionally distinctive beyond coffee.
